definition: I2S substitutes the binary file-format magic bytes at the start of the input so the program's format-dispatch branch routes into the correct parser; without the magic the input is treated as a different format and the entire branch-under-study is unreachable.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

members.function: bloaty::Bloaty::ScanAndRollupFile(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, bloaty::Rollup*, std::vector<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >, std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > > >*) const

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S splices the Mach-O magic bytes and section-name literals intercepted from format-dispatch CMPs directly into the input, enabling build_id extraction that loser WASM-magic inputs can never reach.
signature.one_line: I2S supplies Mach-O magic and section bytes to yield a non-empty build_id

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a Mach-O object file whose magic and section structure let bloaty parse a non-empty build_id.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The leading bytes must form a Mach-O magic constant (ce fa ed fe / cf fa ed fe) followed by enough valid Mach-O load-command/section structure (including __ section-name bytes 5f 5f) that file->GetBuildId() returns a non-empty string, so the true side of if (!build_id.empty()) is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe (or cf fa ed fe) and carry embedded 5f 5f (__) section-name bytes and load-command sizing fields; these parse into a Mach-O whose build_id (UUID) is recovered, populating build_id and exercising lines 1751-1757 only in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d (WASM \0asm) or similar non-Mach-O magic; bloaty parses them as a format that yields an empty build_id, so GetBuildId() returns empty and line 1750 always takes the false side (T=0 in L).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the object-file format CMPs (magic and structural-field comparisons) and I2SRandReplace splices the exact required literals (Mach-O magic, section-name bytes) into the input at the offsets where the parser compares them; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct the multi-field magic/structure by blind havoc, so they never produce a parseable build_id.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side requires a non-empty build_id at the if guard.
build_id is produced by parsing the object file via file->GetBuildId().
Only winner-resolving seeds exercise the true-side body lines 1751-1757.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1750  T=54 F=0  T=0 F=120  if (!build_id.empty()) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe and carry embedded __ (5f 5f) section-name bytes.
Another winner seed uses the alternate Mach-O 64-bit magic cf fa ed fe.
Loser-blocking seeds begin with non-Mach-O magic such as 00 61 73 6d (\0asm).
At the leading magic offsets W and L bytes are sharply concentrated and disjoint (ce/fa/ed/fe vs 00/61/73/6d).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets, unlike naive.

scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; only its vpc counterpart adds it, matching the second decisive pair direction.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a non-empty build_id behind a Mach-O magic/structure check in which naive (no I2S) resolves the branch at the same rate as cmplog within the budget, showing the magic/structure literals are reachable by blind havoc rather than requiring I2S substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: That bloaty's GetBuildId() returns non-empty specifically for Mach-O (vs other formats) is inferred from the seed magic bytes and source guard, not directly observed from a build_id value in the prompt.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S surfaces the ELF magic to keep the memcmp guard passing, while also exposing that the byte at EI_DATA must avoid both case-label values; cmplog reaches the default arm by landing a non-{1,2} byte that naive keeps setting to a matching case value.
signature.one_line: I2S preserves ELF magic while placing a non-case byte at EI_DATA to hit default

analysis.summary_one_line: Valid ELF magic at bytes 0-3 plus byte 0x05 (EI_DATA) holding a value other than 1 (LSB) or 2 (MSB) to reach the default arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes 0x00-0x03 must equal the literal magic 7f 45 4c 46 (\177ELF) so the memcmp guard at line 452 passes, AND byte 0x05 (ident[EI_DATA]) must hold a value other than ELFDATA2LSB(1) or ELFDATA2MSB(2) so the switch at line 468 falls through to the default arm at line 475.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seeds keep the magic 7f 45 4c 46 at offsets 0x00-0x03 and place a non-{1,2} value at offset 0x05 (e.g. seed 1 has 0x30, seed 2 has 0x00, seed 3 has 0xe7), so EI_DATA misses both case labels and hits default.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds also keep the magic but set offset 0x05 to either 0x01 or 0x02 in every sample, so EI_DATA always matches case ELFDATA2LSB or ELFDATA2MSB and the switch never reaches default.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLog buffer for the switch comparison on ident[EI_DATA] and substitutes the recorded compared operand at the input offset, but unlike a single magic-number gate the winning move is to make the byte NOT equal the case labels; I2S surfaces the magic prefix (7f 45 4c 46) keeping line 452 satisfied while the switch on a single byte is escaped via a non-matching value, a combination cmplog reaches and naive's blind havoc rarely lands while preserving the magic.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of the switch on ident[EI_DATA] at elf.cc:475.
Reaching the default requires passing the magic memcmp guard and missing both EI_DATA case labels.
The switch dispatches on ident[EI_DATA] with cases ELFDATA2LSB and ELFDATA2MSB before default.
Winning cmplog seeds preserve the ELF magic 7f 45 4c 46 at offsets 0x00-0x03.
A winning seed places a non-{1,2} value (0xe7) at offset 0x05 so EI_DATA misses both case labels.
Loser naive seeds set offset 0x05 to 0x01 or 0x02 (a valid EI_DATA case), so the switch matches a case and never reaches default.
The byte-diff shows W offset 0x05 concentrated on non-{1,2} values while L offset 0x05 is dominated by 01/02.
The divergent-branches table shows the blocker default at line 475 taken only in W (T=60) and never in L (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 475  T=60 F=0  T=0 F=2220  default:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other CMP operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose switch dispatches on a single byte gated behind a preceding magic memcmp showing naive resolving the default arm as often as cmplog (no cmplog>naive divergence), which would mean I2S substitution of the magic prefix is not what enables the winner.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether I2S's advantage here is solving the magic memcmp prefix (a roadblock) versus the switch byte being escaped by chance, since reaching default requires a NON-matching byte that I2S does not directly synthesize.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S reads the logged magic==MH_MAGIC CMP operand and splices the exact 4-byte Mach-O magic into input offset 0x00-0x03, directly satisfying the equality gate naive never blindly hits.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes 4-byte Mach-O magic at offset 0 to pass the format gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with the 4-byte Mach-O magic cf fa ed fe so magic equals MH_MAGIC/MH_MAGIC_64/FAT_CIGAM.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes of the input must equal the Mach-O magic word cf fa ed fe at offset 0x00-0x03 so that the read magic compares equal to one of MH_MAGIC / MH_MAGIC_64 / FAT_CIGAM at macho.cc:618, taking the true branch into the MachOObjectFile constructor.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every cmplog winner-resolving seed begins with the exact bytes cf fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03, which is the little-endian Mach-O magic; the per-offset byte sets show these four offsets are fully concentrated to that value across all 10 winner seeds.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d ('\0asm', the WebAssembly magic), so magic never matches any Mach-O constant and execution falls through to the nullptr return at line 623, exercising the false branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the logged CMP operands from the magic==MH_MAGIC comparison and splices the constant magic value into the input at the matching offset; naive has no CMP introspection and cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte magic, so it never crosses the gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch at macho.cc:618 fires the true side only when magic equals one of three Mach-O magic constants.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row I2S signal is a lower-bound floor at depth 3 of one seed's chain; it confirms some I2S contribution but does not prove the specific magic-byte splice was the I2S edit rather than a later havoc refinement.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S intercepts the format-magic comparison, logs the Mach-O magic operand, and substitutes it at offset 0x00-0x03, routing the input into the Mach-O reader where the FindContaining true branch becomes reachable.
signature.one_line: I2S writes Mach-O magic at byte 0 to enter the parser that exposes the gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry the Mach-O magic ce-fa-ed-fe at offset 0 so the Mach-O parser populates range maps reached by FindContaining.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first four bytes must equal the Mach-O magic number ce fa ed fe (little-endian 0xFEEDFACE). Bloaty's format dispatch matches this magic to enter the Mach-O object reader, which calls AddRange / FindContaining repeatedly; without the magic match the input is routed to a different (e.g. WASM) parser whose path drives FindContaining only on the false side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with ce fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03; the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe (and a few ASCII M/Z/L/E/P/F) at those offsets, and the winners reach FindContaining 562 times and its callers far more, with the blocker true side taken (T=101/160).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed begins with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM magic '.asm'), so the Mach-O reader is never entered; FindContaining is still invoked 1720 times on the L set but the blocker true side is taken 0 times (T=0 F=1720), i.e. the loser parser path never produces the empty-result condition the true branch encodes.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the format-magic comparison and log both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the seed's leading bytes and substitutes the expected magic operand (ce fa ed fe) directly at offset 0. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble onto the exact 4-byte magic by chance via havoc, which is why naive never resolves (0/10) and value_profile resolves only 1/10.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique and both name I2S as the delta.
The cmplog>naive pair is also an I2S delta.
The blocked side is the true branch of FindContaining's empty-result test.
All winner-resolving seeds start with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe at offset 0.
The W byte set at offsets 0x00-0x03 is concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe while the L byte set is the WASM magic 00 61 73 6d.
Every loser-blocking seed starts with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM '.asm' magic).
On the loser set FindContaining's blocker true side is taken 0 times despite 1720 invocations.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, so its only route to the exact magic is chance havoc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: naive never resolved the branch and value_profile resolved it only once across ten trials.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates FindContaining behind a 4-byte magic compare shows naive resolving the true branch as often and as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), meaning the magic match is not the controlling feature.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the Mach-O magic at offset 0 is the specific gate (rather than some other 4-byte constant elsewhere in the header) is inferred from the seed-prefix divergence, not from a directly cited source-level magic comparison.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S reads CMP operands from ELF header field comparisons and substitutes the exact magic and structural field values, enabling the translator map to be populated and CoversRange's miss-path to be exercised.
signature.one_line: I2S reconstructs ELF header fields to drive translator map and reach CoversRange true side

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed ELF whose parsed structural fields build a translator map then query CoversRange with an address outside that map.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An ELF input (magic 7f 45 4c 46) whose header/structural fields parse far enough to populate translator_ and invoke RangeMap::CoversRange via RangeSink::AddRange (bloaty.cc:1331), supplying a vmaddr/vmsize whose first lookup iterator either reaches mappings_.end() or fails EntryContains, so the else-if at range_map.cc:315 takes its true side and returns false.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds carry a clean ELF magic plus concentrated structural bytes (e.g. 7f ff-marked fields at 0x09-0x0c and the PU/0x50 0x55 pair at 0x18-0x19) that satisfy the parser CMP gates so the translator_ branch is entered and CoversRange is reached, where the W-only lines 316-317 (return false) are hit and the branch true side covered.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds carry malformed/space-filled bytes after the magic (runs of 0x20 across 0x0a-0x1c, swapped ELF class/data at 0x04-0x06) that never drive the parser through the translator_-establishing path with an uncovered address; their CoversRange invocations stay on the false side (line 315 L T=0 F=1260000) and the branch true side is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted integer/string CMP operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected operand into matching input offsets, letting it reconstruct the exact ELF header field values that the bloaty parser compares against; naive must hit those multi-byte field values by blind havoc and never does within budget, so only cmplog drives the input through the translator map construction that exercises the CoversRange true side.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of an else-if in CoversRange that returns false when the iterator is at end or EntryContains fails.
The winning side covers the return-false lines 316-317 (W-only) while the loser only ever exercises the surrounding loop.
CoversRange is reached through the translator_ guard at the call site bloaty.cc:1331.
Winner-resolving cmplog seeds begin with a clean ELF magic and concentrated structural bytes including the 50 55 pair at offset 0x18.
Loser-blocking naive seeds fill the post-magic region with runs of 0x20 spaces rather than valid structural fields.
W and L bytes diverge sharply across offsets 0x06-0x1c where the winner holds concentrated values and the loser holds spaces, the dataflow signal into the parser fields.
On the loser side the blocker true side is taken zero times against 1260000 false on the d=1 enclosing frame.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact ELF field whose CMP gate I2S solves to enter the translator_ path is inferred from byte-diff concentration and source structure, not directly traced to a logged CMP operand.

signature.mechanism_summary: cmplog/vpc intercept the 4-byte sfnt-version tag comparison, log Typ1Tag ('typ1') as the expected operand, and I2SRandReplace substitutes it at offset 0x00-0x03, satisfying the switch case that naive/vp cannot hit blind.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes 4-byte sfnt tag 'typ1' at offset 0 to clear switch case

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with a recognized 4-byte SFNT tag (e.g. "typ1") at offset 0 so u.tag matches a non-collection case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes (offset 0x00-0x03) must equal a recognized non-collection SFNT signature tag such as Typ1Tag ('typ1' = 74 79 70 31), so that the switch on u.tag in get_face takes the case Typ1Tag/TrueTypeTag arm instead of falling to default.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the literal bytes 74 79 70 31 ('typ1'); the byte-diff shows W is fully concentrated on t/y/p/1 at offsets 0x00-0x03 (each 20/20), matching the Typ1Tag case.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry arbitrary leading bytes (00/e0/ff/87...) at offsets 0x00-0x03 that match no tag case, so the switch falls to default and returns Null, blocking the true side (L hits 491 default, never 487).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the tag comparison and record both operands in the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the expected tag literal (Typ1Tag/etc.) into the input at the matching offset, planting the 4-byte magic that naive/value_profile must hit by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case Typ1Tag arm of a switch on u.tag inside get_face, whose true side is globally blocked.
The branch dispatches on the 4-byte tag value u.tag with sibling tag cases and a default fallthrough.
All five winner-resolving seeds start with the literal bytes 74 79 70 31 spelling 'typ1'.
At offsets 0x00-0x03 the winner bytes are fully concentrated on t/y/p/1 (20 of 20) while loser bytes are a scattered set of other values.
Loser-blocking seeds carry arbitrary leading bytes that do not spell a tag, e.g. a seed beginning 87 0e 0d 0e 22 0e.
In the loser the switch falls to the default arm (line 491 returns Null) rather than the Typ1Tag arm.

picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ from their loser by exactly the I2S technique.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive/value_profile resolve the Typ1Tag case at the same rate as cmplog/vpc despite the gate requiring a 4-byte exact tag match, showing I2S substitution is not what plants the magic bytes.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The cmplog winner's lineage shows only one dash row (depth 3) and the leaf op-list is havoc/token, so the I2S contribution is a lower bound and could partly be lucky havoc.

members.source_line: case CFFTag:	/* All the non-collection tags */

signature.mechanism_summary: cmplog/vpc log the 32-bit CFFTag ('OTTO') constant from the sfnt-version switch, then I2SRandReplace substitutes those exact 4 bytes at offset 0, directly manufacturing the magic that neither naive nor value_profile lands blind.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes 4-byte 'OTTO' magic at offset 0 to satisfy sfnt-version switch

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with a valid OpenType sfnt-version FOURCC (e.g. 'OTTO' / TrueType) so the tag switch hits a non-collection case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 input bytes must equal a recognized OpenType sfnt-version magic tag (CFFTag 'OTTO', TrueTag, Typ1Tag, or TrueTypeTag) so the switch on u.tag at line 511 reaches the case at line 512 instead of falling through to default at line 518.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the 4 bytes 4f 54 54 4f ('OTTO'); the BYTE DIFF shows offsets 0x00-0x03 are concentrated on exactly 4f/54/54/4f across all winners, matching the CFFTag literal the switch compares against.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry scattered, non-magic leading bytes (e.g. 87 0e 0d 0e, 00 00 00 00, e0 17 00 00); none of them equal an sfnt-version tag, so u.tag falls to default (line 518) and the true side of the switch case is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the 32-bit tag comparison and log the constant operand (the sfnt-version magic) into CmpLogObserver; I2SRandReplace then substitutes that exact 4-byte constant into the input at the matching offset, manufacturing 'OTTO' at offset 0 directly rather than waiting for blind havoc to land 4 specific bytes. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and never resolve it.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is a switch case on the parsed tag, and the true (winning) side is globally blocked.
